zh.Holme, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾霍爾梅
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme於1270年出生在英國林可爾史爾霍爾梅時，他的父親John Holmes22歲，母親Anconetta de la Twyer36歲。他和Joan St. Quinton至少有3個兒子。他去世於1324年在他的家鄉享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Holme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northwestern England): variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.
Danish and Swedish: variant of   Holm   1.

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Holmes
meanings: English: either from the plural form of Holme , or else a variant of Holme or Home , with excrescent -s (see Holm ).

Scottish: probably a habitational name from Holmes near Dundonald (Ayrshire), or possibly from another place so called in the barony of Inchestuir (Angus). Both placenames likely derive from the plural form of Middle English, Older Scots holm ‘islet, raised land in a marsh’ (see Holm ).

Scottish and Irish: adopted for Scottish Gaelic and Irish Mac Thómais, Mac Thómais (see McComb ). In parts of western Ireland, Holmes is also a variant of Cavish, from Gaelic Mac Thámhais, another patronymic from Thomas . Early bearers in Ireland were probably immigrants from Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme was born in 1270, in Holme, Lincolnshire, England, his father, John Holmes, was 22 and his mother, Anconetta de la Twyer, was 36. He had at least 3 sons with Joan St. Quinton. He died in 1324, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme est né en 1270, à Holme, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, John Holmes, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Anconetta de la Twyer, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Joan St. Quinton. Il est décédé en 1324, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme 1270 in Holme, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Holmes 22 und seine Mutter Anconetta de la Twyer 36. Er hatte mit Joan St. Quinton mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1324 im Alter von 54 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme è nato il nel 1270 nel Holme, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, John Holmes, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Anconetta de la Twyer, aveva 36 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Joan St. Quinton. Morì nel 1324, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme(이)가 1270년 영국 링코실르 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Holmes(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Anconetta de la Twyer(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Joan St. Quinton(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1324년 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme nasceu em 1270, em Holme, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Holmes, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Anconetta de la Twyer, tinha 36 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Joan St. Quinton. Ele faleceu em 1324, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme родился в 1270 г. в местности холм, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Holmes было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Anconetta de la Twyer, 36. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Joan St. Quinton. Он умер в 1324 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Holme of Paull Holme nació en 1270, en Holme, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Holmes, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Anconetta de la Twyer, tenía 36 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Joan St. Quinton. Murió en 1324, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.
